schema.name: TZPID source truth generated from canonical equation master

theory.canonical_statement: The mathematical structure of equatorial wave propagation bears striking resemblance to geodesic deviation in curved spacetime. The wave operator governing equatorial dynamics: \begin{equation} \mathcal{L} = \partial_t^2 - c^2\nabla^2 + f^2 - \beta y \partial_x \end{equation}
theory.technical_interpretation: Connection to Geodesic Deviation: L is a lagrangian construction, written L = ∂_t^2 - c^2∇^2 + f^2 - β y ∂_x. It defines a Lagrangian density from which the dynamics follow by the principle of least action. It is built from ∇, β, c, f, y, x, defining L. Within the Trawin Zero Point registry it serves as one element of the framework's mathematical narrative.
